nysOpenData 0.1.2

List available New York State Open Data datasets

Source: R/nys_list_datasets.R
nys_list_datasets.Rd

Retrieves a live catalog of New York State Open Data datasets from the New York State Open Data Portal catalog table (`ca8h-8gjq`) and returns dataset metadata that can be used with [nys_pull_dataset()].

Usage

nys_list_datasets()

Value

A tibble containing available New York State Open Data datasets. Important columns include:

key

A human-readable dataset key generated from the dataset name.

uid

The official Socrata dataset identifier used by New York State Open Data.

name

The dataset name from the New York State Open Data catalog.

Additional metadata columns from the New York State Open Data catalog may also be returned.

Details

The returned tibble includes both the official Socrata dataset `uid` and a human-readable `key`. The `uid` is the stable identifier used by the New York State Open Data Portal and Socrata API, while the `key` is generated from the dataset name using [janitor::make_clean_names()] to make datasets easier to reference in R code, especially in classroom and reproducible research settings.

Most users will begin by calling `nys_list_datasets()`, searching the returned catalog for a dataset of interest, and then passing either the `key` or `uid` to [nys_pull_dataset()].

Examples

if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {
  catalog <- nys_list_datasets()

  # View available datasets
  catalog

  # Search for datasets containing a keyword
  catalog[
    grepl("KEYWORD", catalog$name, ignore.case = TRUE),
    c("key", "uid", "name")
  ]
}
